articles.headline: Anduril Raises $5 Billion, as Push to Modernize the Military Accelerates
articles.summary: Defense tech startup Anduril doubled its valuation to $61 billion in a $5B funding round, signaling accelerated investment in AI-backed weapons amid US military modernization. This boosts demand for AI hardware and software suppliers while highlighting geopolitical tensions affecting global tech supply chains and export controls. Corporations in chips, cloud, and enterprise AI may see new contract opportunities or compliance risks.

articles.headline: Wall Street sees 'changing of the guard in AI' as Intel, AMD shares soar while Nvidia lags
articles.summary: Intel, AMD, and Micron shares surged double digits as investors shift bets to CPU makers and memory firms powering next-stage AI infrastructure, with Nvidia lagging. This market reaction signals changing demand dynamics for chips and memory, potentially lowering costs for AI deployments in enterprises. Businesses evaluating hardware investments should note opportunities in diversified suppliers amid volatile Nvidia dominance.

articles.headline: 5 Business Technology Trends Leaders Can't Ignore in 2026
articles.summary: Agentic AI is shifting to autonomous workflows at Walmart and AstraZeneca, automating payroll, research, and merchandising for major efficiency gains. Robotics-as-a-service and small language models (SLMs) are mainstreaming, promising cost reductions for SMEs via focused, cheaper AI over large models. Executives must prioritize FinOps for cloud costs and proactive AI cybersecurity to manage rising operational impacts.
